live_path.role: CLI entrypoint that parses --owner and forwards it into the service call.
Request orchestrator that resolves owner metadata before record creation, routing-key construction, and payload emission.
Derives effective owner and owner_source from explicit CLI input or the default-owner fallback.
Passes the resolved owner into storage for base-record construction only.
Builds routing_key from the already-resolved owner and request name.
Emits the final payload by combining the base record with owner_source and routing_key.

logic: If owner is present and non-blank, return owner.strip() with owner_source='explicit'.
Otherwise load config/defaults.json::owner via sync_app/service.py::_load_defaults and return owner_source='default'.

routing_key.ordering: sync_app/service.py::sync_item computes routing_key after owner resolution and after base-record creation, not before CLI owner precedence.

emission.copy_behavior: serialize_payload copies the record and appends emitted metadata fields.

test_observations.observation: sync_item('Launch Checklist', 'pending', owner='pm-oncall') returns owner='pm-oncall', owner_source='explicit', and routing_key='pm-oncall:launch-checklist'.
sync_item('Launch Checklist', 'pending') falls back to config/defaults.json::owner and emits owner_source='default' with a routing key built from that owner.
sync_app/cli.py::main forwards --owner into sync_item and the emitted JSON preserves owner_source and routing_key.
The artifact contract requires schema_version, variant_id, live_path, field_derivations, test_observations, and rejected_decoys.

rejected_decoys.claim: owner_source in the exported payload comes from storage because owner already exists on the returned record.
rejected_decoys.why_rejected: sync_app/store.py::make_record stores only name/status/owner; sync_app/service.py::_resolve_owner decides owner_source and sync_app/serializer.py::serialize_payload appends it later.

rejected_decoys.claim: routing_key is probably computed before the CLI applies --owner.
rejected_decoys.why_rejected: sync_app/cli.py::main passes args.owner into sync_app/service.py::sync_item, which resolves the owner before calling sync_app/serializer.py::build_routing_key.

rejected_decoys.claim: Storage decided owner_source and routing_key predated owner precedence.
rejected_decoys.why_rejected: incident_context/rollback_note.md says this draft repeated a stale theory without tracing the live call chain.

rejected_decoys.claim: The old support explanation could be reused if tests still passed.
rejected_decoys.why_rejected: The note is explicitly labeled as an unfinished draft and is not backed by the live call chain in sync_app/cli.py::main and sync_app/service.py::sync_item.

rejected_decoys.claim: A store helper might be the live source of owner_source.
rejected_decoys.why_rejected: Repo-local search shows no call sites for this helper; sync_app/service.py imports only sync_app/store.py::make_record.

rejected_decoys.claim: Routing-key construction may still live in the store layer.
rejected_decoys.why_rejected: Repo-local search shows no call sites for this helper; the live path uses sync_app/serializer.py::build_routing_key.

rejected_decoys.claim: The emitted owner_source might be drafted back out of the stored record.
rejected_decoys.why_rejected: Repo-local search shows no call sites for this helper; the live path passes owner_source directly from sync_app/service.py::_resolve_owner into sync_app/serializer.py::serialize_payload.

rejected_decoys.claim: Serializer planning notes describe the current implementation.
rejected_decoys.why_rejected: The file says it is design intent only and does not assert that the live code already moved owner attribution into serializer.
